title: Ibn Battuta — through the Anatolian beyliks

intro.title: Among the Akhi Brotherhoods
intro.body: Ibn Battuta crossed Anatolia in winter 1331-1332, lodging among the akhi brotherhoods of the post-Seljuk beyliks. The Rihla devotes a long chapter to the journey from Alanya in the south to Sinope on the Black Sea coast.

stops.name: Alanya
stops.note: Landfall from Latakia, autumn 1331. Battuta describes the harbour and the Karamanid emir's hospitality.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Seljuk red-towered harbour, traveller in scholar's robe greeted on a quay by turbaned officials. red brick tower by the harbour.

stops.name: Konya
stops.note: Old Seljuk capital. Battuta visits the tomb of Mevlana Rumi (d. 1273) and lodges with the akhis.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Conical green-tiled mausoleum of Rumi above an old town, dervishes in white robes turning in courtyard.

stops.name: Kayseri
stops.note: Eretnid territory. Battuta describes the great hospitality of the akhis and his lodging in their convent.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Anatolian highland town under snow, akhi convent with its banner outside, evening lamplight.

stops.name: Sivas
stops.note: Battuta witnesses an akhi turf-war in Sivas — two brotherhoods almost come to blows over who will host him.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Carved-portal medrese gate, two akhi delegations facing across a snowy street, traveller between.

stops.name: Sinope
stops.note: Black Sea port. Battuta waits forty days for fair sailing to the Crimea, then embarks for Caffa.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Black Sea harbour under low cloud, single Genoese galley in the bay, scholar standing on the mole.
